This likely means that Unity has filled Odin's stored serialization data with garbage, which can randomly happen after upgrading the Unity version of the project, or when otherwise doing things that have a lot of fragile interactions with the asset database. Locating the asset which causes this error log and causing it to reserialize (IE, modifying it and then causing it to be saved to disk) is likely to 'fix' the issue and make this message go away. Experience shows that this issue is particularly likely to occur on prefab instances, and if this is the case, the parent prefab is also under suspicion, and should be re-saved and re-imported. Note that DATA MAY HAVE BEEN LOST, and you should verify with your version control system (you're using one, right?!) that everything is alright, and if not, use it to rollback the asset to recover your data.

Binary is recommended for builds; JSON has the benefit of being human-readable but has significantly worse performance.
With the special editor-only node format, the serialized data will be formatted in such a way that, if the asset is saved with Unity's text format (Edit -> Project Settings -> Editor -> Asset Serialization -> Mode), the data will be mergeable when using version control systems. This makes the custom serialized data a lot less fragile, but comes at a performance cost during serialization and deserialization. The node format is recommended in the editor.
This setting can be overridden on a per-instance basis.

'LoadComplete.Audio.Manager|AudioManager
Odin's custom serialization protocol is stable and fast. It is built to be fast, reliable and resilient above all.
*Words of caution* 
However, caveats apply - there is a reason Unity chose such a drastically limited serialization protocol. It keeps things simple and manageable, and limits how much complexity you can introduce into your data structures. It can be very easy to get carried away and shoot yourself in the foot when all limitations suddenly disappear, and hence we have included this cautionary warning.
Warning words aside, there can of course be valid reasons to use a more powerful serialization protocol such as Odin's. However, we advise you to use it wisely and with restraint. After all, with great power comes great responsibility!
In 2018.3, Unity introduced a new prefab workflow, and in so doing, changed how all prefabs fundamentally work. Despite our best efforts, we have so far been unable to achieve a stable implementation of Odin-serialized prefab modifications on prefab instances and variants in the new prefab workflow.This has nothing to do with Odin serializer itself, which remains rock solid. Odin-serialized ScriptableObjects and non-prefab Components/Behaviours are still perfectly stable - you are only seeing this message because this is an Odin-serialized prefab asset or instance.
Using prefabs with Odin serialization in 2018.3 and above is considered a *deprecated feature* and is officially unsupported. In short, if you disregard this message and then experience issues, we will not be able to help or support you.
Please keep all this in mind, if you wish to continue using Odin-serialized prefabs.bI know what I'm about, son. Hide message forever.lI understand that I'm on my own. Hide message forever.

Filters out pixels under this level of brightness. Value is in gamma-space.
:Strength of the bloom filter.
FSet the radius of the bloom effect.
Set the maximum intensity that Unity uses to calculate Bloom. If pixels in your Scene are more intense than this, URP renders them at their current intensity, but uses this intensity value for the purposes of Bloom calculations.
Use the color picker to select a color for the Bloom effect to tint to.

fA 2D Lookup Texture (LUT) to use for color grading.
How much of the lookup texture will contribute to the color grading effect.
Use "Gaussian" for a faster but non physical depth of field; "Bokeh" for a more realistic but slower depth of field.
\The distance at which the blurring will start.
The distance at which the blurring will reach its maximum radius.
The maximum radius of the gaussian blur. Values above 1 may show under-sampling artifacts.
Use higher quality sampling to reduce flickering and improve the overall blur smoothness.
FThe distance to the point of focus.
The ratio of aperture (known as f-stop or f-number). The smaller the value is, the shallower the depth of field is.
The distance between the lens and the film. The larger the value is, the shallower the depth of field is.
<The number of aperture blades.
,The curvature of aperture blades. The smaller the value is, the more visible aperture blades are. A value of 1 will make the bokeh perfectly circular.
VThe rotation of aperture blades in degrees.
The type of grain to use. You can select a preset or provide your own texture by selecting Custom.
xUse the slider to set the strength of the Film Grain effect.
Controls the noisiness response curve based on scene luminance. Higher values mean less noise in light areas.
A tileable texture to use for the grain. The neutral value is 0.5 where no grain is applied.
0Total distortion amount.
Intensity multiplier on X axis. Set it to 0 to disable distortion on this axis.
Intensity multiplier on Y axis. Set it to 0 to disable distortion on this axis.
pDistortion center point. 0.5,0.5 is center of the screen
Controls global screen scaling for the distortion effect. Use this to hide screen borders when using high "Intensity."
Use this to control and apply a hue to the dark tones. This has a more exaggerated effect on shadows.
Use this to control and apply a hue to the mid-range tones with a power function.
Use this to increase and apply a hue to the signal and make highlights brighter.
The motion blur technique to use. If you don't need object motion blur, CameraOnly will result in better performance.
The quality of the effect. Lower presets will result in better performance at the expense of visual quality.
The strength of the motion blur filter. Acts as a multiplier for velocities.
RSets the maximum length, as a fraction of the screen's full resolution, that the velocity resulting from Camera rotation can have. Lower values will improve performance.

<Panini projection crop to fit.

Select a tonemapping algorithm to use for the color grading process.
Specifies the range reduction mode used when HDR output is enabled and Neutral tonemapping is enabled.
JUse the ACES preset for HDR displays.
Specify how much hue to preserve. Values closer to 0 are likely to preserve hue. As values get closer to 1, Unity doesn't correct hue shifts.
\Enable to use values detected from the output device as paper white. When enabled, output images might differ between SDR and HDR. For best accuracy, set this value manually.
zThe reference brightness of a paper white surface. This property determines the maximum brightness of UI. The brightness of the scene is scaled relative to this value. The value is in nits.
4Enable to use the minimum and maximum brightness values detected from the output device. For best accuracy, considering calibrating these values manually.
The minimum brightness of the screen (in nits). This value is assumed to be 0.005f with ACES Tonemap.
The maximum brightness of the screen (in nits). This value is defined by the preset when using ACES Tonemap.
Vignette color.

Should the vignette be perfectly round or be dependent on the current aspect ratio?

Sets the white balance to compensate for a green or magenta tint.
`Decals will affect only base color and emission.
pDecals will affect only base color, normal and emission.
Decals will affect base color, normal, metallic, ambient occlusion, smoothness and emission.
pAutomatically selects technique based on build platform.
DBuffer
bRenders decals into DBuffer and then applied during opaque rendering. Requires DepthNormal prepass which makes not viable solution for the tile based renderers common on mobile.
Renders decals after opaque objects with normal reconstructed from depth. The decals are simply rendered as mesh on top of opaque ones, as result does not support blending per single surface data (etc. normal blending only).
jLow quality of normal reconstruction (Uses 1 sample).
rMedium quality of normal reconstruction (Uses 5 samples).
nHigh quality of normal reconstruction (Uses 9 samples).

ZShaders unconditionally support HDR decoding.
BatchCullingContext.isOrthographic is deprecated. Use BatchCullingContext.projectionType instead.
Directional lights support cookies now. In order to position the cookie projection in the world, a position and full orientation are necessary. Use the position and orientation members instead of the direction parameter.
Enum member GraphicsFormat.RGB_DXT1_SRGB has been deprecated. Use GraphicsFormat.RGBA_DXT1_SRGB instead (UnityUpgradable) -> RGBA_DXT1_SRGB
Enum member GraphicsFormat.RGB_DXT1_UNorm has been deprecated. Use GraphicsFormat.RGBA_DXT1_UNorm instead (UnityUpgradable) -> RGBA_DXT1_UNorm
|Enum member GraphicsFormat.DepthAuto has been deprecated. Use GraphicsFormat.None as a color format to indicate depth only rendering and DefaultFormat to get the default depth buffer format.
Enum member GraphicsFormat.ShadowAuto has been deprecated. Use GraphicsFormat.None as a color format to indicate depth only rendering and DefaultFormat to get the default shadow buffer format.
Enum member GraphicsFormat.VideoAuto has been deprecated. Use DefaultFormat instead.
Future versions of Unity are expected to always throw exceptions and not have this field.

hDetermines how UI elements in the Canvas are scaled.
If a sprite has this 'Pixels Per Unit' setting, then one pixel in the sprite will cover one unit in the UI.
hScales all UI elements in the Canvas by this factor.
The resolution the UI layout is designed for. If the screen resolution is larger, the UI will be scaled up, and if it's smaller, the UI will be scaled down. This is done in accordance with the Screen Match Mode.
A mode used to scale the canvas area if the aspect ratio of the current resolution doesn't fit the reference resolution.
Determines if the scaling is using the width or height as reference, or a mix in between.
hThe physical unit to specify positions and sizes in.
bThe DPI to assume if the screen DPI is not known.
The pixels per inch to use for sprites that have a 'Pixels Per Unit' setting that matches the 'Reference Pixels Per Unit' setting.
The amount of pixels per unit to use for dynamically created bitmaps in the UI, such as Text.

This option is obsolete and will have no effect - a GlobalConfig will always have an asset generated now; use a POCO singleton or a ScriptableSingleton<T> instead. Asset-less config objects that are recreated every reload cause UnityEngine.Object leaks.

VUse of this API is highly discouraged, it selects legacy-mode LM/NTLM authentication, which sends your password in very weak encryption over the wire even if the server supports the more secure NTLMv2 / NTLMv2 Session. You need to use the new `Type3Message (Type2Message)' constructor to use the more secure NTLMv2 / NTLMv2 Session authentication modes. These require the Type 2 message from the server to compute the response.

BUnity.InternalAPIEngineBridge.001
bUnity.MonoScriptGenerator.MonoScriptInfoGenerator
XThe down sampling rate for soft mask buffer.
 m_DesamplingRate
The value used by the soft mask to select the area of influence defined over the soft mask's graphic.
ZThe transparency of the whole masked graphic.
 Advanced Options
\Should the soft mask ignore parent soft masks?
XIs the soft mask a part of parent soft mask?
fSelf graphic will not be drawn to soft mask buffer.

.UnityEngine.VideoModule
 Warning messages
<Click to show warning message.
Odin's custom serialization protocol is stable and fast. It is built to be fast, reliable and resilient above all.
*Words of caution* 
However, caveats apply - there is a reason Unity chose such a drastically limited serialization protocol. It keeps things simple and manageable, and limits how much complexity you can introduce into your data structures. It can be very easy to get carried away and shoot yourself in the foot when all limitations suddenly disappear, and hence we have included this cautionary warning.
Warning words aside, there can of course be valid reasons to use a more powerful serialization protocol such as Odin's. However, we advise you to use it wisely and with restraint. After all, with great power comes great responsibility!U
<Click to show warning message.
In 2018.3, Unity introduced a new prefab workflow, and in so doing, changed how all prefabs fundamentally work. Despite our best efforts, we have so far been unable to achieve a stable implementation of Odin-serialized prefab modifications on prefab instances and variants in the new prefab workflow.This has nothing to do with Odin serializer itself, which remains rock solid. Odin-serialized ScriptableObjects and non-prefab Components/Behaviours are still perfectly stable - you are only seeing this message because this is an Odin-serialized prefab asset or instance.
Using prefabs with Odin serialization in 2018.3 and above is considered a *deprecated feature* and is officially unsupported. In short, if you disregard this message and then experience issues, we will not be able to help or support you.
Please keep all this in mind, if you wish to continue using Odin-serialized prefabs.U
BEnabling this will hide all warning messages that will show up in the inspector when the OdinSerialize attribute potentially does not achieve the desired effect.U
\Enabling this will hide all warning messages that show up when the SerializeField and the ShowInInspector attributes are used together on non-serialized fields or properties.U
Hide Non-Serialized SerializeField/ShowInInspector Warning Messages
